untranslatable_reason: This signal is a meta-rule about entry concept validation (win rate >= 52% across 200+ backtests) rather than a price-action rule expressible with technical indicators. It describes a backtesting validation threshold and risk management principle, not tradeable entry/exit conditions on price, volume, or oscillators. Cannot be translated into indicator-based conditions without access to strategy performance metrics (win_rate, sample_size) which are not in the allowed indicator list.
translation_notes: THARP_DRY_13_2 is a gating rule for strategy acceptance, not a direct entry signal. The signal states: reject any entry concept with <52% win rate (or 55% per book text) over 1-5 day timeframes and minimum 200 trades. This is a meta-level filter applied before trade execution, requiring backtest statistics rather than real-time market data. Implementation requires: (1) Backtest the entry concept over >=200 trades, (2) Calculate win_rate across 1-5 day holds, (3) Only deploy live if win_rate >= 0.52, (4) Monitor live trading and revert if reliability drops below threshold. The source text mentions 45-55% reliability range as marginally above random, supporting the 52-55% threshold.
